query: "gas prices" OR "gasoline prices" OR "oil prices" OR petrol

articles.title: Ethanol blending won't lower fuel rates: Ex-HPCL chief busts ‘myth’
articles.description: The E20 petrol debate has ignited conversations across sectors. At this time, Surana dismissed the "myth" that ethanol blending should lower fuel prices.  | India News

articles.title: Pakistan To Revise Petroleum Prices Daily As Government Shifts To New Fuel Pricing System
articles.description: OGRA will determine daily fuel prices based on a seven-day international average, while petrol pump owners warn of protests against the deregulation policy.

articles.title: Five new driving rules and laws on table with Andy Burnham as Prime Minister
articles.description: MP for Makerfield is being urged to make a string of changes which experts say would be welcomed by petrol, diesel AND electric motorists.

articles.title: Erupting Conflict: Iran and US Escalate Gulf Tensions
articles.description: Iran's IRGC warns nations hosting US troops of retaliatory actions amid escalating Gulf tensions, as American and Iranian forces exchange strikes affecting key strategic locations. The ongoing conflict intensifies around the Strait of Hormuz, impacting global oil prices and regional stability.

articles.title: Pakistan to revise oil prices daily amid renewed West Asia tensions
articles.description: Pakistan has announced that petroleum prices in the country will be adjusted daily to tackle the uneven fluctuation in oil prices triggered by the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

articles.title: US warns Americans to reconsider Middle East travel amid rising tensions
articles.description: The United States issued a travel warning for the Middle East due to escalating regional tensions. Escalating hostilities between the United States and Iran involved fresh military strikes. Fighting intensified over the Strait of Hormuz, impacting global oil prices significantly. Kuwait and Bahrain reported missile and drone interceptions amid the widening conflict. Commercial vessel crossings dropped, while Iran maintained its maritime access demands.

articles.title: Longer-dated yields lower after data, on track for weekly decline
articles.description: Longer-dated U.S. Treasury yields declined on Friday after recent economic data. Markets largely priced out a Federal Reserve rate hike for this month. Import prices increased unexpectedly, while consumer and producer prices showed moderation. Geopolitical worries and hawkish Fed comments added to market uncertainty. Bond yields moved lower, suggesting a flight to safety amid rising oil prices.

articles.title: Oil settles up on renewed US-Iran hostilities and threat of Red Sea closure
articles.description: Oil prices climbed significantly on Friday, reaching their highest point in over a month. Escalating hostilities between the United States and Iran intensified attacks across the Gulf region. Shipping routes face potential closure, impacting global oil flows and prices. Saudi Arabia has redirected significant crude exports to avoid the Strait of Hormuz. Ukraine also reported striking a Russian oil refinery in its conflict.

articles.title: Tech selloff weighs on European shares ahead of ECB meeting next week
articles.description: European stocks edged lower Friday, influenced by a global tech sell-off and Middle East tensions. Investors await the European Central Bank meeting and corporate earnings reports next week. Rising oil prices and subdued reactions to strong results complicated the market backdrop. Tech stocks experienced a significant decline this week, impacting major AI beneficiaries. Utilities and defense sectors saw gains, while luxury brands faced headwinds from reduced tourist spending.

articles.title: 'Not anti consumer, but pro India': JSW Group's Sajjan Jindal as E20 blending fears grip motorists
articles.description: IIT Kanpur researchers said extensive tests found no evidence that E20 petrol damages vehicle engines. The findings challenge online claims as concerns over mileage, wear and legal disputes continue.
